title: Large stack allocation in enqueue_next_pending depends on config
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshcore/adapter.c
Line: 360

```c
uint8_t out[LICHEN_MESHCORE_FRAME_MAX];
```

This allocates LICHEN_MESHCORE_FRAME_MAX bytes on the stack, where the size depends on CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_FRAME.

On embedded systems with limited stack sizes (often 1-8KB), a large CONFIG value (e.g., 4096 bytes) could cause stack overflow when this function is called from an interrupt handler or deeply nested call chain.

Consider:
1. Adding BUILD_ASSERT(LICHEN_MESHCORE_FRAME_MAX <= 256, "Frame too large for stack allocation");
2. Using static allocation with mutex protection
3. Passing a caller-provided buffer

Similar concern exists in encode_pending_text() which receives 'out' and 'out_len' from this function.
close_reason: Replaced stack allocation of LICHEN_MESHCORE_FRAME_MAX in enqueue_next_pending() with pre-allocated adapter->tx_buf (option 3 from bead). Verified no functional change to encode/enqueue path. Updated .beads/ files.
